perturbation.original_text: 1. DEFINITIONS. (a) "CONTENT" means all content or information, in any medium, provided by a party to the other party for use in conjunction with the performance of its obligations hereunder, including without limitation any text, music, sound, photographs, video, graphics, data or software. Content provided by 2TheMart is referred to herein as "2TheMart Content" and Content provided by i-Escrow is referred to herein as "i-Escrow Content."
perturbation.changed_text: 1. DEFINITIONS. (a) "CONTENT" means all content or information, in any medium, provided by a party to the other party for use in conjunction with the performance of its obligations hereunder, including only text and images. Content provided by 2TheMart is referred to herein as "2TheMart Content" and Content provided by i-Escrow is referred to herein as "i-Escrow Content."
perturbation.explanation: This change limits the definition of 'CONTENT' to only text and images. This contradicts Section 6.1 which refers to publicly performing and digitally performing the 2TheMart Content which implies that the content can contain items besides text and images.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 CONTENT. 2TheMart hereby grants to i-Escrow a worldwide, non-exclusive right to use, reproduce, distribute, publicly perform, publicly display and digitally perform the 2TheMart Content soley with respect to and in conjunction with the Co-Branded Site all with the prior written consent of 2TheMart, for the term of this Agreement. i-Escrow hereby grants to 2TheMart a worldwide, non-exclusive right to use, reproduce, distribute, publicly perform, publicly display and digitally perform the i-Escrow Content on or in conjunction with 2TheMart auctions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'CONTENT' to include 'only text and images.' This directly contradicts Section 6.1, which grants rights to 'publicly perform' and 'digitally perform' the 2TheMart Content. These actions imply that the content may include audio, video, or other media beyond just text and images, creating a strong in-text contradiction. The definition's limitation directly conflicts with the permitted uses described in section 6.1.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall continue for one (1) year following the Launch Date, unless earlier terminated as provided herein. This Agreement may be renewed for any number of successive one (1) year terms by mutual written agreement of the parties prior to the conclusion of the term of this Agreement. A party wishing to renew this Agreement shall give the other party notice thereof no less than thirty (30) days before the expiration of the term then in effect. In the event that either party does not give such notice, the term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed for another one (1) year.
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall continue for one (1) year following the Launch Date, unless earlier terminated as provided herein. This Agreement may be renewed for any number of successive one (1) year terms by mutual written agreement of the parties prior to the conclusion of the term of this Agreement. A party wishing to renew this Agreement shall give the other party notice thereof no less than thirty (30) days before the expiration of the term then in effect. In the event that either party does not give such notice, the term of this Agreement shall not be automatically renewed, and shall be subject to renegotiation.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the automatic renewal and adding that the contract is subject to renegotiation, this contradicts the previous statement that the contract can be renewed by written agreement. This creates an ambiguity about how the contract can be renewed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 TERM. The term of this Agreement shall continue for one (1) year following the Launch Date, unless earlier terminated as provided herein. This Agreement may be renewed for any number of successive one (1) year terms by mutual written agreement of the parties prior to the conclusion of the term of this Agreement. A party wishing to renew this Agreement shall give the other party notice thereof no less than thirty (30) days before the expiration of the term then in effect. In the event that either party does not give such notice, the term of this Agreement shall not be automatically renewed, and shall be subject to renegotiation.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement is automatically renewed for another year if notice is not given. The changed text removes this automatic renewal and states the agreement is subject to renegotiation if notice is not given. This creates a direct conflict regarding the renewal process, as the changed text contradicts the original automatic renewal provision creating a clear contradiction within 8.1 TERM.

perturbation.original_text: 5. PAYMENT. 5.1 ADVERTISING FEES. After the Launch Date, i-Escrow shall pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on the number of Transaction Inquiries. This advertising fees shall consist of a per Transaction Inquiry amount calculated by multiplying 0.025% by the amount of the average Transaction from all Customers in the preceding quarter. The formula for arriving at the per Transaction Inquiry amount may be revised from time to time during the term of this Agreement to reflect present market conditions ("the Adjusted Rate"), but only by mutual consent of the parties after good faith discussions. The Adjusted Rate shall be added as an addendum to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. PAYMENT. 5.1 ADVERTISING FEES. After the Launch Date, i-Escrow shall pay 2TheMart advertising fees based on the number of Transaction Inquiries. This advertising fees shall consist of a per Transaction Inquiry amount calculated by multiplying 0.025% by the amount of the average Transaction from all Customers in the preceding quarter. The formula for arriving at the per Transaction Inquiry amount will be revised from time to time during the term of this Agreement to reflect present market conditions ("the Adjusted Rate"), at the sole discretion of i-Escrow. The Adjusted Rate shall be added as an addendum to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This changes the condition for revising the formula for advertising fees from 'mutual consent of the parties after good faith discussions' to 'at the sole discretion of i-Escrow'. This creates a contradiction as section 12.8 states that 'The parties agree to act in good faith with respect to each provision of this Agreement and any dispute that may arise related hereto.' Giving i-Escrow the sole discretion will lead to bad faith.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.8 GOOD FAITH. The parties agree to act in good faith with respect to each provision of this Agreement and any dispute that may arise related hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 5.1 to grant i-Escrow sole discretion in revising advertising fees, whereas section 12.8 mandates that both parties act in good faith with respect to all provisions. This creates a direct contradiction because granting one party sole discretion conflicts with the obligation to act in good faith, as it removes the need for mutual agreement and good faith discussions. A reasonable reader would immediately recognize the incompatibility between granting sole discretion to i-Escrow and requiring both parties to act in good faith.
